# AI Model Benchmarks 2026

> Community-driven reference for AI model selection: benchmarks, pricing, routing, embeddings.

## What this is

A structured dataset of 119 AI models compared across 48 benchmarks with:
- Per-score freshness dates (every score has a measured date and source URL)
- Auto-updated pricing from OpenRouter API (daily)
- Task routing recommendations (best model for each task at each budget tier)
- Benchmark lifecycle tracking (active, saturated, dead)
- 26 embedding models with MTEB scores

## Why it exists

Most benchmark tables show one score per model, measured at some unknown date. Scores go stale, prices change, new models appear. This repo solves that by tracking *when* each score was measured, *where* it came from, and whether the benchmark itself is still meaningful.

## How to use this data

- **Pick a model for a task:** see data/routing.json → quick_matrix (25 task categories)
- **Compare models:** see data/models.json (119 models with scores, pricing, metadata)
- **Find embeddings:** see data/embeddings.json (26 models with MTEB scores)
- **Understand benchmarks:** see data/benchmarks.json (48 benchmarks with lifecycle status)

## Data format

All data is in JSON files under data/. Every score includes:
- `value` — the benchmark score
- `measured` — date when the score was collected (YYYY-MM or YYYY-MM-DD)
- `source` — URL to the leaderboard or paper

## Trust model

- No self-reported scores from model providers
- 16 independent sources in trust hierarchy (see docs/METHODOLOGY.md)
- Automated validation (scripts/validate.py)
- Full git history as audit trail
